nearby_text: Recalling Equations 6.1, 6.2b, and 6.3b, the response in trait value, $ R_z = \overline{z}' - \overline{z} $, becomes $$ \begin{aligned}R_{z}&=\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\prime}\overline{z}_{i}-\sum_{i}q_{i}z_{i}\\&=\left(\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\prime}z_{i}-\sum_{i}q_{i}z_{i}\right)+\left(\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\prime}\overline{z}_{i}-\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\prime}z_{i}\right)\\&=\sum_{i}\left(q_{i}^{\prime}-q_{i}\right)z_{i}+\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\prime}\left(\overline{z}_{i}-z_{i}\right)\\&=\sum_{i}\Delta q_{i}z_{i}+\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\prime}\overline{\delta}_{i}\\ \end{aligned} $$ (6.4)

label: Formula 6.15a
section: PRICE'S GENERAL THEOREM OF SELECTION
subsection: The Breeder's Equation, $ R = h^{2} S $
nearby_text: number of copies of allele $ j $ in individual $ i $ (values of 0, 1, or 2), so that (for a diploid) $ \overline{x}_j / 2 = p_j $, the frequency of allele $ j $. The average of Equation 6.14 becomes $$ \overline{z}=\sum_{j}b_{j}\overline{x}_{.j}=\sum_{j}2b_{j}p_{j} $$ (6.15a)

label: Formula 6.16d
section: Theorems of Natural Selection: Results of Price, Fisher, and Robertson: Introduction
subsection: The Breeder's Equation, $ R = h^{2} S $
nearby_text: Following selection (but before reproduction), only QQ and Qq survive, each with a trait value of one, yielding the fitness-weighted mean $$ \mu^{*}=1\cdot\frac{1}{\overline{W}}\left(1-p^{2}\right)+0\cdot\frac{2}{\overline{W}}p^{2}=\frac{1-p^{2}}{\overline{W}}=\frac{1-p^{2}}{1+p^{2}} $$ (6.16d) yielding the selectional differential $$ S=\mu^{*}-\mu=\frac{1-p^{2}}{1+p^{2}}-\left(1-p^{2}\right)=-p^{2}\frac{1-p^{2}}{1+p^{2}} $$ (6.16e)

label: Formula 6.17a
section: FISHER'S FUNDAMENTAL THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION
subsection: The Classical Interpretation of Fisher's Fundamental Theorem, $ R_w = \sigma_A^2(w) $
nearby_text: 2 S $, to predict the response to selection on that trait. Letting $ z = W $, and recalling that $ w = W/\overline{W} $, the Robertson-Price identity (Equation 6.10) yields the selection differential $$ \begin{align*}S_W=\sigma(z,w)=\sigma(W,w)=\frac{\sigma(W,W)}{\overline{W}}=\frac{\sigma^2(W)}{\overline{W}}\end{align*} $$ (6.17a)

section: FISHER'S FUNDAMENTAL THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION

nearby_text: ing, and define $ \overline{W}(p) $ to be the mean population fitness at allele frequency $ p $ (Equation 5.1a). The change in mean fitness is a function of the allele-frequency change, $ \Delta p $, $$ R_{W}=\overline{W}(p+\Delta p)-\overline{W}(p) $$ (6.18a)

label: Formula 6.18c
section: FISHER'S FUNDAMENTAL THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION
subsection: The Classical Interpretation of Fisher's Fundamental Theorem, $ R_w = \sigma_A^2(w) $
nearby_text: If the allele-frequency change is small, a first-order Taylor-series approximation yields $$ \overline{W}(p+\Delta p)\simeq\overline{W}(p)+\frac{d\overline{W}}{d p}\Delta p $$ (6.18b) so $$ R_{W}=\overline{W}(p+\Delta p)-\overline{W}(p)\simeq\frac{d\overline{W}}{d p}\Delta p $$ (6.18c) From Equation 5.5a $$ \begin{aligned}\frac{dW}{dp}&=2pW_{AA}+2(1-2p)W_{Aa}+2(p-1)W_{aa}\\&=2\left[p(W_{AA}-W_{Aa})+(1-p)(W_{Aa}-W_{aa})\right]=2(\alpha_{A}-\alpha_{a})\end{aligned} $$ (6.18d) where t

section: FISHER'S FUNDAMENTAL THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION
subsection: The Classical Interpretation of Fisher's Fundamental Theorem, $ R_w = \sigma_A^2(w) $
nearby_text: Applying Wright’s formula (Equation 5.5b) together with Equation 6.18d returns $$ \Delta p=\frac{p(1-p)}{2\overline{W}}\frac{d\overline{W}}{dp}=\frac{p(1-p)}{2\overline{W}}(2\alpha) $$ (6.18f) and substitution into Equation 6.18c then yields $$ R_{W}\simeq\frac{p(1-p)(2\alpha)^{2}}{2\overline{W}}=\frac{\sigma_{A}^{2}(W)}{\overline{W}} $$ (6.18g)

        "label": "Formula 6.18f",
        "section": "FISHER'S FUNDAMENTAL THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION",

        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}R_{z}&=\\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\\prime}\\overline{z}_{i}-\\sum_{i}q_{i}z_{i}\\\\&=\\left(\\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\\prime}z_{i}-\\sum_{i}q_{i}z_{i}\\right)+\\left(\\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\\prime}\\overline{z}_{i}-\\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\\prime}z_{i}\\right)\\\\&=\\sum_{i}\\left(q_{i}^{\\prime}-q_{i}\\right)z_{i}+\\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\\prime}\\left(\\overline{z}_{i}-z_{i}\\right)\\\\&=\\sum_{i}\\Delta q_{i}z_{i}+\\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\\prime}\\overline{\\delta}_{i}\\\\ \\end{aligned}",

Specifically, $$ R_{W}=\left(\overline{W}^{\prime}\mid E^{\prime}\right)-\left(\overline{W}\mid E\right) $$ (6.22a) where the prime denotes the fitness or environment in the next generation.         "latex": "\\begin{aligned}R_{z}&=\\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\\prime}\\overline{z}_{i}-\\sum_{i}q_{i}z_{i}\\\\&=\\left(\\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\\prime}z_{i}-\\sum_{i}q_{i}z_{i}\\right)+\\left(\\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\\prime}\\overline{z}_{i}-\\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\\prime}z_{i}\\right)\\\\&=\\sum_{i}\\left(q_{i}^{\\prime}-q_{i}\\right)z_{i}+\\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\\prime}\\left(\\overline{z}_{i}-z_{i}\\right)\\\\&=\\sum_{i}\\Delta q_{i}z_{i}+\\sum_{i}q_{i}^{\\prime}\\overline{\\delta}_{i}\\\\ \\end{aligned}",

label: Formula 6.23
section: IMPLICATIONS OF FISHER'S THEOREM FOR TRAIT VARIATION
subsection: Traits Correlated With Fitness Have Lower Heritabilities
nearby_text: nce (environmental and genetic), makes the additive variance in fitness $ \beta^{2}\sigma_{A}^{2} $. The heritability of fitness can be expressed in terms of the variance components for z as follows: $$ \begin{align*}h_W^2=\frac{\beta^2\sigma_A^2}{\beta^2\left(\sigma_A^2+\sigma_E^2\right)+\sigma_e^2}=\frac{\sigma_A^2}{\sigma_A^2+\sigma_E^2+\sigma_e^2/\beta^2}<h_z^2=\frac{\sigma_A^2}{\sigma_A^2+\sigma_E^2}\end{align*} $$ (6.23)

label: Formula 6.24a
section: ROBERTSON'S SECONDARY THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION

nearby_text: y theorem of natural selection states that the change in any character produced by a selection process is equal to the additive covariance between fitness and the character itself.” In equation form, $$ R_{z}=\sigma_{A}(w,z)=\sigma(A_{w},A_{z}) $$ (6.24a)

label: Formula 6.24b
section: ROBERTSON'S SECONDARY THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION
subsection: 1968 Version: $ R_{z} = \sigma_{A}(w, z) $
nearby_text: breeding value, $ \Delta A_z $, which in turn is the change in the breeding value of relative fitness, $ R_w = \Delta A_w $ times the regression of the trait breeding value $ A_z $ given $ A_w $, or $$ R_{z}=\Delta A_{z}=\beta_{A_{z}|A_{w}}\Delta A_{w} $$ (6.24b) From standard regression theory (LW Chapter 3), the slope of the regression of $ A_{z} $ on $ A_{w} $ is simply $$ \beta_{A_{z}|A_{w}}=\frac{\sigma(A_{w},A_{z})}{\sigma^{2}(A_{w})} $$ (6.24c)

        "latex": "h^{2}=\\frac{\\sigma_{A}^{2}}{\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma_{D}^{2}}=\\frac{2p^{3}(1-p)}{2p^{3}(1-p)+p^{2}(1-p)^{2}}=\\frac{2p}{1+p}",
        "label": "Formula 6.16c",
        "section": "Theorems of Natural Selection: Results of Price, Fisher, and Robertson: Introduction",
        "subsection": "The Breeder's Equation, $ R = h^{2} S $",
        "position": 28,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.16d",
        "latex": "\\mu^{*}=1\\cdot\\frac{1}{\\overline{W}}\\left(1-p^{2}\\right)+0\\cdot\\frac{2}{\\overline{W}}p^{2}=\\frac{1-p^{2}}{\\overline{W}}=\\frac{1-p^{2}}{1+p^{2}}",
        "label": "Formula 6.16d",
        "section": "Theorems of Natural Selection: Results of Price, Fisher, and Robertson: Introduction",

        "position": 29,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.16e",
        "latex": "S=\\mu^{*}-\\mu=\\frac{1-p^{2}}{1+p^{2}}-\\left(1-p^{2}\\right)=-p^{2}\\frac{1-p^{2}}{1+p^{2}}",
        "label": "Formula 6.16e",
        "section": "Theorems of Natural Selection: Results of Price, Fisher, and Robertson: Introduction",
        "subsection": "The Breeder's Equation, $ R = h^{2} S $",
        "position": 30,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {

        "latex": "R_{z}=h^{2}S=\\left(\\frac{2p}{1+p}\\right)\\left(-p^{2}\\frac{1-p^{2}}{1+p^{2}}\\right)=-\\frac{2p^{3}(1-p)}{1+p^{2}}",
        "label": "Formula 6.16f",
        "section": "Theorems of Natural Selection: Results of Price, Fisher, and Robertson: Introduction",
        "subsection": "The Breeder's Equation, $ R = h^{2} S $",

        "latex": "\\begin{align*}S_W=\\sigma(z,w)=\\sigma(W,w)=\\frac{\\sigma(W,W)}{\\overline{W}}=\\frac{\\sigma^2(W)}{\\overline{W}}\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula 6.17a",
        "section": "FISHER'S FUNDAMENTAL THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION",
        "subsection": "The Classical Interpretation of Fisher's Fundamental Theorem, $ R_w = \\sigma_A^2(w) $",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.17b",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}R_W=h_W^2S_W={\\sigma_A^2(W)\\over\\sigma^2(W)} {\\sigma^2(W)\\over\\overline{W}}={\\sigma_A^2(W)\\over\\overline{W}}\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula 6.17b",
        "section": "FISHER'S FUNDAMENTAL THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION",
        "subsection": "The Classical Interpretation of Fisher's Fundamental Theorem, $ R_w = \\sigma_A^2(w) $",

        "label": "Formula 6.17c",
        "section": "FISHER'S FUNDAMENTAL THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION",
        "subsection": "The Classical Interpretation of Fisher's Fundamental Theorem, $ R_w = \\sigma_A^2(w) $",

        "label": "Formula 6.20b",

  "nearby_text": "breeding value, $ \\Delta A_z $, which in turn is the change in the breeding value of relative fitness, $ R_w = \\Delta A_w $ times the regression of the trait breeding value $ A_z $ given $ A_w $, or $$ R_{z}=\\Delta A_{z}=\\beta_{A_{z}|A_{w}}\\Delta A_{w} $$ (6.24b) From standard regression theory (LW Chapter 3), the slope of the regression of $ A_{z} $ on $ A_{w} $ is simply $$ \\beta_{A_{z}|A_{w}}=\\frac{\\sigma(A_{w},A_{z})}{\\sigma^{2}(A_{w})} $$ (6.24c)"

nearby_text: To see this connection, taking the regression of breeding value on phenotype as $ A_z = h^2(z - \mu) + e_A $ and substituting into Equation 6.25a yields $$ R_{z}=\sigma\left[w,h^{2}(z-\mu)+e_{A}\right]=h^{2}\sigma(w,z)+\sigma(w,e_{A})=h^{2}S+\sigma(w,e_{A}) $$ (6.25b) and we recover the breeder’s equation when $ \sigma(w, e_A) = 0 $. Biologically, this last assumption implies that the relative fitness of an individual is uncorrelated with the residual error, $ e_A

  "nearby_text": "To see this connection, taking the regression of breeding value on phenotype as $ A_z = h^2(z - \\mu) + e_A $ and substituting into Equation 6.25a yields $$ R_{z}=\\sigma\\left[w,h^{2}(z-\\mu)+e_{A}\\right]=h^{2}\\sigma(w,z)+\\sigma(w,e_{A})=h^{2}S+\\sigma(w,e_{A}) $$ (6.25b) and we recover the breeder’s equation when $ \\sigma(w, e_A) = 0 $. Biologically, this last assumption implies that the relative fitness of an individual is uncorrelated with the residual error, $ e_A"
}

label: Formula 6.25c
section: Theorems of Natural Selection: Results of Price, Fisher, and Robertson: Introduction
subsection: 1966 Version: $ R_{z} = \sigma(w, A_{z}) $
nearby_text: fferent from the residual $ e_A $ from Equation 6.25b, which is the error in predicting trait breeding value given trait phenotypic value). Substituting this expression for w in Equation 6.25a yields $$ R_{z}=\sigma(w,A_{z})=\sigma(A_{w}+e_{w},A_{z})=\sigma(A_{w},A_{z})+\sigma(e_{w},A_{z}) $$ (6.25c) showing that while the 1966 version is more general, the two versions (Equations 6.24a and 6.25a) are equal when $ \sigma(e_w, A_z) = 0 $.

section: ROBERTSON'S SECONDARY THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION
subsection: Connecting Robertson’s Results With Those of Price, Fisher, and Lush
nearby_text: ning that $ \bar{\delta} $ is not zero but is still small, then Robertson's (1966a) theorem is a good approximation for the response to selection. If we write $ w = A_w + e_w $, Equation 6.28 becomes $$ \begin{aligned}\Delta\overline{A}_{z}&=\sigma(A_{w}+e_{w},A_{z})+\sigma(w,\overline{\delta})+E(\overline{\delta})\\&=\sigma(A_{w},A_{z})+\sigma(e_{w},A_{z})+\sigma(w,\overline{\delta})+E(\overline{\delta})\end{aligned} $$ (6.29) which recovers Robertson’s 1968 version (Equation 6.25a), provided that the last three terms are zero (or else are very small relative to the first term).

        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\Delta\\overline{A}_{z}&=\\sigma(w,A_{z}+\\overline{\\delta})+E(\\overline{\\delta})\\\\&=\\sigma(w,A_{z})+\\sigma(w,\\overline{\\delta})+E(\\overline{\\delta})\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 6.28",
        "section": "ROBERTSON'S SECONDARY THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION",
        "subsection": "Connecting Robertson’s Results With Those of Price, Fisher, and Lush",
        "position": 67,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      }

  },
  "nearby_text": "ning that $ \\bar{\\delta} $ is not zero but is still small, then Robertson's (1966a) theorem is a good approximation for the response to selection. If we write $ w = A_w + e_w $, Equation 6.28 becomes $$ \\begin{aligned}\\Delta\\overline{A}_{z}&=\\sigma(A_{w}+e_{w},A_{z})+\\sigma(w,\\overline{\\delta})+E(\\overline{\\delta})\\\\&=\\sigma(A_{w},A_{z})+\\sigma(e_{w},A_{z})+\\sigma(w,\\overline{\\delta})+E(\\overline{\\delta})\\end{aligned} $$ (6.29) which recovers Robertson’s 1968 version (Equation 6.25a), provided that the last three terms are zero (or else are very small relative to the first term)."

  "nearby_text": "decomposed into contributions from two biologically interpretable linear regressions and their corresponding residuals. The first regression involves offspring $ (z_i) $ and parental $ (z_i) $ values $$ \\begin{array}{r l}{\\overline{{z}}_{i}=\\mu+\\beta_{\\overline{{z}}|z}z_{i}+e_{\\overline{{z}},i},\\quad\\mathrm{w h e r e}\\quad\\beta_{\\overline{{z}}|z}=\\frac{\\sigma(\\overline{{z}}_{i},z_{i})}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}}\\end{array} $$ (6.30b)"
}

label: Formula 6.30c
section: THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION
subsection: Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection
nearby_text: The second is the regression of relative fitness $ w_{i} $ of a parent i on its phenotypic value $ z_{i} $ $$ w_{i}=1+\beta_{w|z}z_{i}+e_{w,i},\quad\mathrm{w h e r e}\quad\beta_{w|z}=\frac{\sigma(w_{i},z_{i})}{\sigma_{z}^{2}}=\frac{S}{\sigma_{z}^{2}} $$ (6.30c)

section: THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION
subsection: Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection
nearby_text: yields $$ \sigma(x,y||z)=\sigma(e_{x},e_{y})=\sigma\left[x-\left(\frac{\sigma(x,z)}{\sigma_{z}^{2}}\right)z,y-\left(\frac{\sigma(y,z)}{\sigma_{z}^{2}}\right)z\right] $$ which, by expansion, leads to $$ \begin{aligned}\sigma(x,y||z)&=\sigma(x,y)-\left(\frac{\sigma(x,z)}{\sigma_{z}^{2}}\right)\sigma(y,z)-\left(\frac{\sigma(y,z)}{\sigma_{z}^{2}}\right)\sigma(x,z)+\left(\frac{\sigma(y,z)}{\sigma_{z}^{2}}\right)\left(\frac{\sigma(x,z)}{\sigma_{z}^{2}}\right)\sigma_{z}^{2}\\&=\sigma(x,y)-\frac{\sigma(x,z)\sigma(y,z)}{\sigma_{z}^{2}}\quad\end{aligned} $$ (6.33d)

label: Formula 6.33e
section: THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION

nearby_text: The last term can be expressed as either $ \beta_{x|z}\sigma(y,z) $ or $ \beta_{y|z}\sigma(x,z) $.       {
        "id": "formula_6.33a",
        "latex": "\\sigma(x,y\\mid z)=E\\Biggl[\\biggl(x-E(x\\mid z)\\biggr)\\biggl(y-E(y\\mid z)\\biggr)\\mid z\\Biggr]",
        "label": "Formula 6.33a",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",

      {
        "id": "formula_6.33b",
        "latex": "\\sigma(x,y||z)=\\sigma(e_{x},e_{y})",

        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.33c",
        "latex": "e_{x}=x-\\left(\\mu_{x}+\\beta_{x|z}z\\right)=x-\\mu_{x}-\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)z",
        "label": "Formula 6.33c",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 78,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "e",
          "x"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.33d",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\sigma(x,y||z)&=\\sigma(x,y)-\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\sigma(y,z)-\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(y,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\sigma(x,z)+\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(y,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\\\&=\\sigma(x,y)-\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)\\sigma(y,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\quad\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 6.33d",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 79,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]

      {
        "id": "formula_6.33e",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z})-\\frac{\\sigma(\\overline{z},z)}{\\sigma_{\\overline{z}}^2}\\sigma(w,z)=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z})-\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}\\sigma(w,z)\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula 6.33e",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 80,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{z}",
          "\\sigma",
          "w",
          "z"
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "nearby_text": "Rearranging this last expression yields $$ \\begin{aligned}\\sigma(w,\\overline{z})&=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)+\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}\\sigma(w,z)\\\\&=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)+\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}S\\end{aligned} $$ (6.33f) which is used to obtain Equation 6.32."
}

label: Formula 6.34
section: THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION
subsection: Parent-Offspring Regressions Before and After Selection
nearby_text: \overline{z}_i^\circ $ as the difference in the offspring means for parent $ i $ when crossed to selected versus unselected parents. Because $ \overline{z}_i = \overline{z}_i^\circ + \delta_i^\circ $ $$ \sigma(\overline{z}_{i},z_{i})=\sigma(\overline{z}_{i}^{\circ}+\delta_{i}^{\circ},z_{i})=\sigma(\overline{z}_{i}^{\circ},z_{i})+\sigma(\delta_{i}^{\circ},z_{i}) $$ (6.34)

  "nearby_text": "\\overline{z}_i^\\circ $ as the difference in the offspring means for parent $ i $ when crossed to selected versus unselected parents. Because $ \\overline{z}_i = \\overline{z}_i^\\circ + \\delta_i^\\circ $ $$ \\sigma(\\overline{z}_{i},z_{i})=\\sigma(\\overline{z}_{i}^{\\circ}+\\delta_{i}^{\\circ},z_{i})=\\sigma(\\overline{z}_{i}^{\\circ},z_{i})+\\sigma(\\delta_{i}^{\\circ},z_{i}) $$ (6.34)"
}

section: THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION

nearby_text: The same logic leading to Equation 6.33f (Example 6.8) can be used to obtain two useful identities: $$ \sigma(w,\overline{{z}}^{\circ})=\sigma(w,\overline{{z}}^{\circ}||z)+\beta_{\overline{{z}}^{\circ}|z}\sigma(w,z) $$ (6.36a) and $$ \sigma(w,\overline{z}||z)=\sigma(w,\overline{z}^{\circ}||z)+\sigma(w,\delta^{\circ})-\frac{\sigma(\delta^{\circ},z)\sigma(w,z)}{\sigma_{z}^{2}} $$ (6.36b)

        "latex": "e_{x}=x-\\left(\\mu_{x}+\\beta_{x|z}z\\right)=x-\\mu_{x}-\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)z",

      {
        "id": "formula_6.33d",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\sigma(x,y||z)&=\\sigma(x,y)-\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\sigma(y,z)-\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(y,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\sigma(x,z)+\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(y,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\\\&=\\sigma(x,y)-\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)\\sigma(y,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\quad\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 6.33d",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",

        "label": "Formula 6.33f",

label: Formula 6.37
section: THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION
subsection: Parent-Offspring Regressions Before and After Selection
nearby_text: Finally, while $ \bar{\delta}_{i} $ is often considered to be the change from parent to offspring in the absence of selection, this is not strictly correct. Formally, it has two components, $$ \overline{\delta}_{i}=\overline{z}_{i}-z_{i}=(\overline{z}_{i}^{\circ}+\delta_{i}^{\circ})-z_{i}=\delta_{i}^{\circ}+(\overline{z}_{i}^{\circ}-z_{i}) $$ (6.37)

        "latex": "\\beta_{A_{z}|A_{w}}=\\frac{\\sigma(A_{w},A_{z})}{\\sigma^{2}(A_{w})}",
        "label": "Formula 6.24c",
        "section": "ROBERTSON'S SECONDARY THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION",

        ]
      },
      {

        "subsection": "1966 Version: $ R_{z} = \\sigma(w, A_{z}) $",

        "symbols_defined": [

          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.25b",
        "latex": "R_{z}=\\sigma\\left[w,h^{2}(z-\\mu)+e_{A}\\right]=h^{2}\\sigma(w,z)+\\sigma(w,e_{A})=h^{2}S+\\sigma(w,e_{A})",
        "label": "Formula 6.25b",
        "section": "Theorems of Natural Selection: Results of Price, Fisher, and Robertson: Introduction",
        "subsection": "1966 Version: $ R_{z} = \\sigma(w, A_{z}) $",

        "symbols_defined": [

      },
      {

        "latex": "R_{z}=\\sigma(w,A_{z})=\\sigma(A_{w}+e_{w},A_{z})=\\sigma(A_{w},A_{z})+\\sigma(e_{w},A_{z})",
        "label": "Formula 6.25c",
        "section": "Theorems of Natural Selection: Results of Price, Fisher, and Robertson: Introduction",
        "subsection": "1966 Version: $ R_{z} = \\sigma(w, A_{z}) $",

        "section": "ROBERTSON'S SECONDARY THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION",
        "subsection": "Accuracy of the Secondary Theorem",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.28",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\Delta\\overline{A}_{z}&=\\sigma(w,A_{z}+\\overline{\\delta})+E(\\overline{\\delta})\\\\&=\\sigma(w,A_{z})+\\sigma(w,\\overline{\\delta})+E(\\overline{\\delta})\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 6.28",
        "section": "ROBERTSON'S SECONDARY THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION",
        "subsection": "Connecting Robertson’s Results With Those of Price, Fisher, and Lush",

        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.29",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\Delta\\overline{A}_{z}&=\\sigma(A_{w}+e_{w},A_{z})+\\sigma(w,\\overline{\\delta})+E(\\overline{\\delta})\\\\&=\\sigma(A_{w},A_{z})+\\sigma(e_{w},A_{z})+\\sigma(w,\\overline{\\delta})+E(\\overline{\\delta})\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 6.29",
        "section": "ROBERTSON'S SECONDARY THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION",
        "subsection": "Connecting Robertson’s Results With Those of Price, Fisher, and Lush",

        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.30a",
        "latex": "R_{z}=\\sigma(w_{i},\\overline{z}_{i})+E(\\overline{\\delta}_{i})",
        "label": "Formula 6.30a",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 70,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "R",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.30b",
        "latex": "\\begin{array}{r l}{\\overline{{z}}_{i}=\\mu+\\beta_{\\overline{{z}}|z}z_{i}+e_{\\overline{{z}},i},\\quad\\mathrm{w h e r e}\\quad\\beta_{\\overline{{z}}|z}=\\frac{\\sigma(\\overline{{z}}_{i},z_{i})}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}}\\end{array}",
        "label": "Formula 6.30b",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 71,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{z}",
          "i"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.30c",
        "latex": "w_{i}=1+\\beta_{w|z}z_{i}+e_{w,i},\\quad\\mathrm{w h e r e}\\quad\\beta_{w|z}=\\frac{\\sigma(w_{i},z_{i})}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}=\\frac{S}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}",
        "label": "Formula 6.30c",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",

        "symbols_defined": [

      {

        "label": "Formula 6.31a",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.31b",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}\\begin{aligned}\\sigma(w,\\overline{z})&=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)+\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}\\;\\sigma(w,z)\\\\&=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)+\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}S\\end{aligned}\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula 6.31b",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 74,
        "symbols_defined": [

      },
      {

        "latex": "\\begin{align*}R_z=\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}S+\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)+E(\\overline{\\delta})\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula 6.32",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 75,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "R",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.33a",
        "latex": "\\sigma(x,y\\mid z)=E\\Biggl[\\biggl(x-E(x\\mid z)\\biggr)\\biggl(y-E(y\\mid z)\\biggr)\\mid z\\Biggr]",
        "label": "Formula 6.33a",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 76,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\sigma",
          "x",
          "y",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.33b",
        "latex": "\\sigma(x,y||z)=\\sigma(e_{x},e_{y})",
        "label": "Formula 6.33b",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 77,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\sigma",
          "x",
          "y",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.33c",
        "latex": "e_{x}=x-\\left(\\mu_{x}+\\beta_{x|z}z\\right)=x-\\mu_{x}-\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)z",
        "label": "Formula 6.33c",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 78,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "e",
          "x"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.33d",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\sigma(x,y||z)&=\\sigma(x,y)-\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\sigma(y,z)-\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(y,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\sigma(x,z)+\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(y,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\\\&=\\sigma(x,y)-\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)\\sigma(y,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\quad\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 6.33d",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 79,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\sigma",
          "x",
          "y",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.33e",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z})-\\frac{\\sigma(\\overline{z},z)}{\\sigma_{\\overline{z}}^2}\\sigma(w,z)=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z})-\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}\\sigma(w,z)\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula 6.33e",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 80,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{z}",
          "\\sigma",
          "w",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.33f",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\sigma(w,\\overline{z})&=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)+\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}\\sigma(w,z)\\\\&=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)+\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}S\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 6.33f",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 81,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{z}",
          "\\sigma",
          "w"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.34",
        "latex": "\\sigma(\\overline{z}_{i},z_{i})=\\sigma(\\overline{z}_{i}^{\\circ}+\\delta_{i}^{\\circ},z_{i})=\\sigma(\\overline{z}_{i}^{\\circ},z_{i})+\\sigma(\\delta_{i}^{\\circ},z_{i})",
        "label": "Formula 6.34",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Parent-Offspring Regressions Before and After Selection",
        "position": 82,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{z}",
          "\\sigma",
          "i",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.35",
        "latex": "\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}=\\frac{\\sigma(\\overline{z},z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}=\\frac{\\sigma(\\overline{z}^{\\circ},z)+\\sigma(\\delta^{\\circ},z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}=\\beta_{\\overline{z}^{\\circ}|z}+\\frac{\\sigma(\\delta^{\\circ},z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}",
        "label": "Formula 6.35",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Parent-Offspring Regressions Before and After Selection",
        "position": 83,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\beta",
          "\\overline{z}",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.36a",
        "latex": "\\sigma(w,\\overline{{z}}^{\\circ})=\\sigma(w,\\overline{{z}}^{\\circ}||z)+\\beta_{\\overline{{z}}^{\\circ}|z}\\sigma(w,z)",
        "label": "Formula 6.36a",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Parent-Offspring Regressions Before and After Selection",
        "position": 84,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{z}",
          "\\sigma",
          "w"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.36b",
        "latex": "\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}^{\\circ}||z)+\\sigma(w,\\delta^{\\circ})-\\frac{\\sigma(\\delta^{\\circ},z)\\sigma(w,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}",
        "label": "Formula 6.36b",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Parent-Offspring Regressions Before and After Selection",
        "position": 85,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{z}",
          "\\sigma",
          "w",
          "z"
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "nearby_text": "Finally, while $ \\bar{\\delta}_{i} $ is often considered to be the change from parent to offspring in the absence of selection, this is not strictly correct. Formally, it has two components, $$ \\overline{\\delta}_{i}=\\overline{z}_{i}-z_{i}=(\\overline{z}_{i}^{\\circ}+\\delta_{i}^{\\circ})-z_{i}=\\delta_{i}^{\\circ}+(\\overline{z}_{i}^{\\circ}-z_{i}) $$ (6.37)"
}

label: Formula 6.39
section: THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION
subsection: Heywood's Decomposition of Response
nearby_text: ecomposition of the selection response. Expressed in terms of a regression using parents before selection ( $ \beta_{\overline{z}^{o},z} $), substituting Equations 6.35–6.38 into Equation 6.32 yields $$ R=\beta_{\overline{{z}}^{\circ}|z}S+\sigma(w,\overline{{z}}^{\circ}||z)+\sigma(w,\delta^{\circ})+E(\delta^{\circ})+E(\overline{{z}}^{\circ}-z) $$ (6.39)

        "subsection": "1966 Version: $ R_{z} = \\sigma(w, A_{z}) $",
        "position": 61,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "R",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.25b",
        "latex": "R_{z}=\\sigma\\left[w,h^{2}(z-\\mu)+e_{A}\\right]=h^{2}\\sigma(w,z)+\\sigma(w,e_{A})=h^{2}S+\\sigma(w,e_{A})",
        "label": "Formula 6.25b",
        "section": "Theorems of Natural Selection: Results of Price, Fisher, and Robertson: Introduction",
        "subsection": "1966 Version: $ R_{z} = \\sigma(w, A_{z}) $",
        "position": 62,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "R",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.25c",
        "latex": "R_{z}=\\sigma(w,A_{z})=\\sigma(A_{w}+e_{w},A_{z})=\\sigma(A_{w},A_{z})+\\sigma(e_{w},A_{z})",
        "label": "Formula 6.25c",
        "section": "Theorems of Natural Selection: Results of Price, Fisher, and Robertson: Introduction",
        "subsection": "1966 Version: $ R_{z} = \\sigma(w, A_{z}) $",
        "position": 63,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "R",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.26",
        "latex": "R_{z}=\\sigma_{A}(z,w)+B",
        "label": "Formula 6.26",
        "section": "ROBERTSON'S SECONDARY THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION",
        "subsection": "Accuracy of the Secondary Theorem",
        "position": 64,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "R",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.27",
        "latex": "R_{z}=\\sigma_{A}(z,w)+O(s^{2})",
        "label": "Formula 6.27",
        "section": "ROBERTSON'S SECONDARY THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION",
        "subsection": "Accuracy of the Secondary Theorem",
        "position": 65,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "R",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.28",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\Delta\\overline{A}_{z}&=\\sigma(w,A_{z}+\\overline{\\delta})+E(\\overline{\\delta})\\\\&=\\sigma(w,A_{z})+\\sigma(w,\\overline{\\delta})+E(\\overline{\\delta})\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 6.28",
        "section": "ROBERTSON'S SECONDARY THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION",
        "subsection": "Connecting Robertson’s Results With Those of Price, Fisher, and Lush",
        "position": 67,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\Delta",
          "\\overline{A}",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.29",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\Delta\\overline{A}_{z}&=\\sigma(A_{w}+e_{w},A_{z})+\\sigma(w,\\overline{\\delta})+E(\\overline{\\delta})\\\\&=\\sigma(A_{w},A_{z})+\\sigma(e_{w},A_{z})+\\sigma(w,\\overline{\\delta})+E(\\overline{\\delta})\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 6.29",
        "section": "ROBERTSON'S SECONDARY THEOREM OF NATURAL SELECTION",
        "subsection": "Connecting Robertson’s Results With Those of Price, Fisher, and Lush",
        "position": 68,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\Delta",
          "\\overline{A}",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.30a",
        "latex": "R_{z}=\\sigma(w_{i},\\overline{z}_{i})+E(\\overline{\\delta}_{i})",
        "label": "Formula 6.30a",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 70,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "R",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.30b",
        "latex": "\\begin{array}{r l}{\\overline{{z}}_{i}=\\mu+\\beta_{\\overline{{z}}|z}z_{i}+e_{\\overline{{z}},i},\\quad\\mathrm{w h e r e}\\quad\\beta_{\\overline{{z}}|z}=\\frac{\\sigma(\\overline{{z}}_{i},z_{i})}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}}\\end{array}",
        "label": "Formula 6.30b",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 71,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{z}",
          "i"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.30c",
        "latex": "w_{i}=1+\\beta_{w|z}z_{i}+e_{w,i},\\quad\\mathrm{w h e r e}\\quad\\beta_{w|z}=\\frac{\\sigma(w_{i},z_{i})}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}=\\frac{S}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}",
        "label": "Formula 6.30c",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 72,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "i",
          "w"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.31a",
        "latex": "\\sigma(e_{w},e_{\\overline{{z}}})=\\sigma(w,\\overline{{z}}||z)",
        "label": "Formula 6.31a",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 73,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{z}",
          "\\sigma",
          "e",
          "w"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.31b",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}\\begin{aligned}\\sigma(w,\\overline{z})&=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)+\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}\\;\\sigma(w,z)\\\\&=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)+\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}S\\end{aligned}\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula 6.31b",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 74,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{z}",
          "\\sigma",
          "w"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.32",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}R_z=\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}S+\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)+E(\\overline{\\delta})\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula 6.32",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 75,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "R",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.33a",
        "latex": "\\sigma(x,y\\mid z)=E\\Biggl[\\biggl(x-E(x\\mid z)\\biggr)\\biggl(y-E(y\\mid z)\\biggr)\\mid z\\Biggr]",
        "label": "Formula 6.33a",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 76,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\sigma",
          "x",
          "y",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.33b",
        "latex": "\\sigma(x,y||z)=\\sigma(e_{x},e_{y})",
        "label": "Formula 6.33b",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 77,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\sigma",
          "x",
          "y",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.33c",
        "latex": "e_{x}=x-\\left(\\mu_{x}+\\beta_{x|z}z\\right)=x-\\mu_{x}-\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)z",
        "label": "Formula 6.33c",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 78,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "e",
          "x"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.33d",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\sigma(x,y||z)&=\\sigma(x,y)-\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\sigma(y,z)-\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(y,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\sigma(x,z)+\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(y,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\\\&=\\sigma(x,y)-\\frac{\\sigma(x,z)\\sigma(y,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\quad\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 6.33d",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 79,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\sigma",
          "x",
          "y",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.33e",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z})-\\frac{\\sigma(\\overline{z},z)}{\\sigma_{\\overline{z}}^2}\\sigma(w,z)=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z})-\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}\\sigma(w,z)\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula 6.33e",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 80,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{z}",
          "\\sigma",
          "w",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.33f",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\sigma(w,\\overline{z})&=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)+\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}\\sigma(w,z)\\\\&=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)+\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}S\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 6.33f",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Partial Covariance and the Spurious Response to Selection",
        "position": 81,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{z}",
          "\\sigma",
          "w"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.34",
        "latex": "\\sigma(\\overline{z}_{i},z_{i})=\\sigma(\\overline{z}_{i}^{\\circ}+\\delta_{i}^{\\circ},z_{i})=\\sigma(\\overline{z}_{i}^{\\circ},z_{i})+\\sigma(\\delta_{i}^{\\circ},z_{i})",
        "label": "Formula 6.34",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Parent-Offspring Regressions Before and After Selection",
        "position": 82,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{z}",
          "\\sigma",
          "i",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.35",
        "latex": "\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}=\\frac{\\sigma(\\overline{z},z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}=\\frac{\\sigma(\\overline{z}^{\\circ},z)+\\sigma(\\delta^{\\circ},z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}=\\beta_{\\overline{z}^{\\circ}|z}+\\frac{\\sigma(\\delta^{\\circ},z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}",
        "label": "Formula 6.35",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Parent-Offspring Regressions Before and After Selection",
        "position": 83,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\beta",
          "\\overline{z}",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.36a",
        "latex": "\\sigma(w,\\overline{{z}}^{\\circ})=\\sigma(w,\\overline{{z}}^{\\circ}||z)+\\beta_{\\overline{{z}}^{\\circ}|z}\\sigma(w,z)",
        "label": "Formula 6.36a",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Parent-Offspring Regressions Before and After Selection",
        "position": 84,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{z}",
          "\\sigma",
          "w"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.36b",
        "latex": "\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}||z)=\\sigma(w,\\overline{z}^{\\circ}||z)+\\sigma(w,\\delta^{\\circ})-\\frac{\\sigma(\\delta^{\\circ},z)\\sigma(w,z)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}",
        "label": "Formula 6.36b",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Parent-Offspring Regressions Before and After Selection",
        "position": 85,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{z}",
          "\\sigma",
          "w",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.37",
        "latex": "\\overline{\\delta}_{i}=\\overline{z}_{i}-z_{i}=(\\overline{z}_{i}^{\\circ}+\\delta_{i}^{\\circ})-z_{i}=\\delta_{i}^{\\circ}+(\\overline{z}_{i}^{\\circ}-z_{i})",
        "label": "Formula 6.37",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Parent-Offspring Regressions Before and After Selection",
        "position": 86,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\overline{\\delta}",
          "i"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_6.39",
        "latex": "R=\\beta_{\\overline{{z}}^{\\circ}|z}S+\\sigma(w,\\overline{{z}}^{\\circ}||z)+\\sigma(w,\\delta^{\\circ})+E(\\delta^{\\circ})+E(\\overline{{z}}^{\\circ}-z)",
        "label": "Formula 6.39",
        "section": "THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Heywood's Decomposition of Response",
        "position": 87,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "R"
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "nearby_text": "Conversely, when expressed in terms of the regression after selection $ (\\beta_{\\overline{z}|z}) $, the response is $$ R=\\beta_{\\overline{{z}},z}S+\\sigma(w,\\overline{{z}}^{\\circ}||z)+\\sigma(w,\\delta^{\\circ}||z)+E(\\delta^{\\circ})+E(\\overline{{z}}^{\\circ}-z). $$ (6.40)"
}

label: Formula 6.41
section: THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FRAMED WITHIN THE PRICE EQUATION
subsection: Heywood's Decomposition of Response
nearby_text: This is obtained from Equation 6.39, using Equation 6.35 and the identity $$ \sigma(w,\delta^{\circ})-\frac{\sigma(\delta^{\circ},z)\sigma(w,z)}{\sigma_{z}^{2}}=\sigma(w,\delta^{\circ}||z) $$ (6.41)
